{{Quote|I had family on Siluria III.|ConJob, to Captain Kaiya Adrimetrum|<ref name="SD" />}}
'''ConJob''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Information broker/Legends|infochant]] and [[florist]] who plied his trade on [[Tanquilla Beach/Legends|''Tanquilla Beach'']], a [[Shadowport/Legends|shadowport]] in the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. Previously a resident of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Siluria III]], ConJob lost his family during an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Occupation of Siluria III|attack]] upon the population. Relocating to ''Tanquilla Beach'', ConJob operated as both a [[Plant/Legends|flower]] [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]] and information broker, carving both into successful businesses.

Well known on the shadowport, ConJob was aware of nearly everything that occurred on the [[Space station/Legends|space station]], and was the main source of information on events happening in the Kathol [[Sector/Legends|sector]]. In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], he met [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Kaiya Adrimetrum]], [[Commander/Legends|commanding]] [[Officer/Legends|officer]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]''. Recognizing her as a former resistance leader on Siluria III who had fought back against the Empire, he assisted her in obtaining help from his friend, the [[Engineer/Legends|engineer]] [[Spang]], to repair the [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] [[Engine/Legends|engines]] of her [[Starship/Legends|vessel]].

==Biography==
===Siluria III===
ConJob, a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], lived on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Siluria III]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] with his family, and worked as a [[Media/Legends|media]] director. The planet's [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Governor/Legends|Governor]],<ref name="SD" /> [[Quannith]],<ref name="Recruitment">{{WEGCite|story=Recruitment Speech|book=Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim}}</ref> treated the population brutally during the Empire's occupation, and ConJob's family was [[Death/Legends|killed]] during a reprisal [[Occupation of Siluria III|attack]] on the population. Leaving Siluria III, ConJob eventually settled on [[Tanquilla Beach/Legends|''Tanquilla Beach'']], a [[Shadowport/Legends|shadowport]] located in the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. Refusing to divulge his past to the residents of the [[Space station/Legends|station]], ConJob allowed rumors about himself to circulate. Some said that he was a [[Professor/Legends|professor]] who had been forced out of civilized [[Realspace/Legends|space]] by the Empire, while others opined that he was a former [[Police officer/Legends|cop]].<ref name="SD" />

===Tanquilla Beach===
[[File:Tanquilla_Beach.jpg|thumb|right|''Tanquilla Beach'', ConJob's home and base of operations]]
On ''Tanquilla Beach'', ConJob opened a small [[florist]] stand named [[ConJob's Trifles]] on the main intercourse of level two, selling [[Plant/Legends|flowers]]—including [[dewy gigglebud]]s and [[starburst plant]]s—that he personally grew in a [[Hydroponics/Legends|hydroponics]] [[Garden/Legends|garden]] located in his quarters. He made a relatively good living as a florist; individuals with nothing but bare walls and rock to look at for [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] on end appreciated the chance to brighten their quarters with ConJob's stock. Even the most hardened criminals on the station were known to purchase his goods. However, the florist stand was merely a front for ConJob's true business as an [[Information broker/Legends|information broker]].<ref name="SD" />

As ''Tanquilla Beach''{{'s}} local infochant and [[NewsNet]] code dealer, ConJob knew the business of everyone visiting the shadowport, and was sure to be aware of anything interesting happening in the [[Sector/Legends|sector]]. His services were expensive, due to the need to utilize a private [[Courier droid/Legends|courier droid]] to capture the public network feeds from [[Gandle Ott]], and he always informed his clients that news from as far away as the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core]] would be [[Standard day/Legends|days]] or even weeks old by the [[Time/Legends|time]] he obtained it. For the right price, ConJob would direct individuals looking to buy goods or services to the right sellers, and he possessed the codes to access [[Cynabar's InfoNet]]; however, he would only sell them to [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] who had been recommended by current subscribers.<ref name="SD" />

===The ''FarStar'' Arrives===
In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], ConJob was working at his stall when [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Kaiya Adrimetrum]], [[Commander/Legends|commanding]] [[Officer/Legends|officer]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'', arrived on the station. Recognizing her as one of the rebels who had opposed Governor Quannith on Siluria III, he immediately approached her and presented her with a starburst plant. Kissing her hand and bowing, he revealed he had had family on Siluria III. Although embarrassed by ConJob's actions, Adrimetrum gracefully accepted his offering. Adrimetrum's [[Starship/Legends|starship]], the ''FarStar'', was in need of a major repair to its [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]], after [[Skirmish at Shintel|suffering]] [[Space warfare/Legends|combat]] damage in the [[Shintel system/Legends|Shintel system]]. The only repair bay that could accommodate the [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]] at ''Tanquilla Beach'' was [[Spang's]], but the proprietor, [[Spang]], was too busy to help them. ConJob, a friend and drinking buddy of Spang's, used his friendship with the [[Engineer/Legends|engineer]] to help Adrimetrum cut a deal for the repairs. Spang allowed his granddaughter, [[Fia (engineer)|Fia]], to conduct the refit, which overhauled and upgraded the ''FarStar''{{'s}} [[Engine/Legends|engines]].<ref name="SD" />

==Personality and traits==
Tall and lanky, ConJob had [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and wore thick [[spectacles]]. Impulsive and romantic, ConJob kept his past a secret from the denizens of ''Tanquilla Beach'', allowing rumors of his former occupations to spread. At some point in his life, he acquired a working antique [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. While he lacked any talent with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], he would practice with the weapon outside his stand to amuse himself, and grew to be quite proficient in basic offensive and defensive moves. He had a skill for growing flowers, and he maintained two separate businesses. He was also skilled in the use of a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]].<ref name="SD" />

As an information broker, ConJob was extremely knowledgeable about events and situations occurring throughout the Kathol sector and aboard ''Tanquilla Beach''. Possessing a working understanding of [[Bureaucrat/Legends|bureaucracy]], ConJob was a skilled businessman and was especially knowledgeable about NewsNets and their operations. He also had an interest in [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Species/Legends|species]], cultures, and [[Language/Legends|languages]]. ConJob was known to [[Gambling/Legends|gamble]], and could [[Programmer/Legends|program]] and repair [[Computer/Legends|computers]] and security equipment.<ref name="SD" />

==Equipment==
ConJob required [[spectacles]], and owned a [[Hold-out blaster/Legends|hold-out blaster]] in addition to his ancient lightsaber. He carried a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]] and maintained a hydroponics garden to grow flowers.<ref name="SD" />

==Behind the scenes==
ConJob appeared in ''[[Shintel Downtime]]'', the fourth [[Roleplaying game|RPG]] adventure in ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'' written by [[Paul Sudlow]] and published in [[1995]]. The gamemaster has the option of using ConJob to supply the players with information regarding the sector and its events should the players return to ''Tanquilla Beach'' following the conclusion of the adventure.
